pullRequest.title: fix: improve path handling for incomplete model file deletions
pullRequest.body:
fixed: https://github.com/gpustack/gpustack/issues/1592#issuecomment-3018260044

After completing the modifications related to resolved_paths, it can be used to accurately obtain the names of files to be deleted, and files that cannot be matched using the '*' wildcard can also be deleted now.

pullRequest.title: refactor(mindie): bump to 2.0.rc1
pullRequest.body:
- update dockerfile
  + bump CANN to 8.1.rc1.beta1
- adjust instance parameters
- compatible mindie 1.0.0 and 2.0.rc1
- revert the vox-box installation introduced by https://github.com/gpustack/gpustack/pull/1919

fix https://github.com/gpustack/gpustack/issues/1997

pullRequest.title: fix: skip meta fetching for custom backends
Fix --oidc-use-userinfo flag
pullRequest.body:
#3565 

This pull request introduces a minor update to the server options configuration in `gpustack/cmd/start.py`. The change fixes support for a OIDC-related option.

- Added the `oidc_use_userinfo` option to the list of supported server options, allowing configuration of whether to use the OIDC UserInfo endpoint. Fixes #3565

pullRequest.title: Fix division by zero in SGLang memory fraction calculation
pullRequest.body:
## Fix Division by Zero Error in SGLang Resource Fit Selector - [x] Analyze the issue: Division by zero occurs when `gpu_mem` is 0 - [x] Fix the division by zero error in `_cal_mem_fraction_static` method - [x] Verify the fix works correctly in isolation - [x] Confirmed existing test failures are due to network issues, not the fix - [x] Code review completed - fix is consistent with existing code patterns - [x] Removed uv.lock changes as requested ### Summary Fixed a `ZeroDivisionError` in the SGLang resource fit selector that occurred when GPU memory was zero. The issue was in the `_cal_mem_fraction_static` method at line 1120, where the code attempted to divide by `gpu_mem` without checking if it was zero. **Change Made:** ```python # Before: _mem_fraction_static = ( round((gpu_mem - reserved_mem) / gpu_mem, 3) if gpu_mem is not None else 0.88 ) # After: _mem_fraction_static = ( round((gpu_mem - reserved_mem) / gpu_mem, 3) if gpu_mem is not None and gpu_mem > 0 else 0.88 ) ``` When `gpu_mem` is 0 (but not None), the code now correctly returns the fallback value of 0.88 instead of attempting the division. ### Testing - ✅ Validated fix works correctly in isolation (no ZeroDivisionError with gpu_mem=0) - ✅ Fix is consistent with existing code patterns (similar check at line 1095) - ❌ Existing tests fail due to network connectivity issues in sandboxed environment (cannot download model configs from HuggingFace), not due to the fix The fix is minimal, surgical, and follows the existing code patterns in the file.
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > An error occurred during evaluation: float division by zero > ### GPUStack version > > v2.0.3rc1 > > ### Operating System & CPU Architecture > > macOS > > ### GPU > > _No response_ > > ### ▶️ Steps to reproduce > > 1. Install GPUStack with embedded worker. > 2. Search for "Qwen/Qwen-Image-2512" on ModelScope and change the backend to SGLang > 3. Check the evaluation result. > > > > ### ❌ Actual result > > > > ``` > 2026-01-09 18:07:18.966032+08:00 - modelscope - INFO - Got 1 files, start to download ... > Downloading Model from https://www.modelscope.cn to directory: /root/.cache/modelscope/hub/models/Tongyi-MAI/Z-Image-Turbo > Downloading Model from https://www.modelscope.cn to directory: /var/lib/gpustack/cache/modelscope/tempfile/Qwen__Qwen-Image-2512__723o_h4w > Downloading [model_index.json]: 100%|██████████| 443/443 [00:00<00:00, 1.03kB/s] > Processing 1 items: 100%|██████████| 1.00/1.00 [00:00<00:00, 2.29it/s] 1.03kB/s] > 2026-01-09 18:07:19.410980+08:00 - modelscope - INFO - Download model 'Qwen/Qwen-Image-2512' successfully. > 2026-01-09 18:07:19.416652+08:00 - gpustack.policies.worker_filters.backend_framework_filter - INFO - BackendFrameworkCompatibilityFilter: 1 workers filtered out > 2026-01-09 18:07:20.530793+08:00 - gpustack.utils.hub - WARNING - The model's config.json does not contain any of the following keys to determine the original maximum length of the model: ['max_position_embeddings', 'n_positions', 'max_seq_len', 'seq_length', 'model_max_length', 'max_target_positions', 'max_sequence_length', 'max_seq_length', 'seq_len']. Assuming the model's maximum length is 2048. > 2026-01-09 18:07:20.531414+08:00 - gpustack.utils.hub - DEBUG - Derived max model length: 2048 > 2026-01-09 18:07:20.532337+08:00 - gpustack.scheduler.evaluator - ERROR - Error evaluating model qwen-image-2512: float division by zero > Traceback (most recent call last): > File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/gpustack/scheduler/evaluator.py", line 151, in evaluate_model_with_cache > result = await evaluate_model(config, session, model, workers) > ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ > File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/gpustack/utils/profiling.py", line 23, in async_wrapper > result = await inner_func(*args, **kwargs) > ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ > File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/gpustack/scheduler/evaluator.py", line 198, in evaluate_model > candidate, schedule_messages = await scheduler.find_candidate( > ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ > File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/gpustack/scheduler/scheduler.py", line 413, in find_candidate > candidates = await candidates_selector.select_candidates(workers) > ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ > File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/gpustack/policies/candidate_selectors/sglang_resource_fit_selector.py", line 320, in select_candidates > await self._cal_mem_fraction_static(workers) > File "/usr/local/lib/python3.11/dist-packages/gpustack/policies/candidate_selectors/sglang_resource_fit_selector.py", line 936, in _cal_mem_fraction_static > round((gpu_mem - reserved_mem) / gpu_mem, 3) > ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~~~ > ZeroDivisionError: float division by zero > 2026-01-09 18:07:20.542729+08:00 - gpustack.utils.profiling - DEBUG - evaluate_models execution time: 2.99 seconds > > ``` > > ### 🔍 Accelerator detection diagnosis (if applicable) > > _No response_ > > ### 🐳 Model deployment / container diagnosis (if applicable) > > _No response_ > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > >

pullRequest.title: Support X-API-Key auth for Anthropic-compatible routes
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- accept `X-API-Key` alongside bearer auth in the incoming API auth path
- forward `x-api-key` through the gateway ext-auth check so Anthropic-compatible clients work unchanged
- add regression tests and document the header in the default CORS allow-list

## Why
Fixes #4882. Claude/Anthropic-compatible tools commonly send API credentials via `X-API-Key`, but GPUStack's incoming auth path only accepted bearer auth. That made Anthropic-compatible routes and gateway auth fail even though GPUStack already uses `X-API-Key` when it talks to Anthropic itself.

## Validation
- added regression coverage for `get_current_user` and worker auth with `X-API-Key`
- ran `python3 -m py_compile` on the touched Python modules with a local pycache prefix
- `pytest` could not be executed in this environment because it is not installed on PATH here
